title: Injury Forces Sonay Kartal to Withdraw from Indian Wells Fourth Round
summary: British tennis player Sonay Kartal was forced to retire from her fourth-round match at the Indian Wells tournament due to a persistent lower back injury. Competing against world number three Elena Rybakina, Kartal received physio treatment throughout the match but lost the first set 6-4. As the pain intensified, she trailed 4-3 in the second set and ultimately decided to withdraw, shaking hands at the net. Kartal, ranked 54th, explained that she had been managing the injury throughout the event but found this match too demanding, stating it was 'one match too many.' Despite not being at her best, she made the contest competitive. This achievement marks her as the first British woman to reach the fourth round at Indian Wells twice. In other results, Rybakina advanced to the quarter-finals to face Jessica Pegula, while Iga Swiatek dominated her match to set up a clash with Elina Svitolina.

background_read: Indian Wells is a prestigious ATP and WTA tennis tournament held annually in California, known for attracting top players. Injuries are common in professional sports, and athletes often have to balance performance with health management. Sonay Kartal's experience highlights the physical demands of tennis and the challenges of competing while injured. Her achievement as the first British woman to reach the fourth round twice at this event underscores her rising career despite setbacks.
Article_Structure: Main Points: The article reports on Sonay Kartal's retirement from the Indian Wells fourth round due to a back injury, her competitive effort against Elena Rybakina, and her significance as the first British woman to achieve this round twice. Purpose: To inform readers about the match outcome, Kartal's injury struggles, and related tournament updates. Evidence Evaluation: Relies on direct quotes from Kartal and factual match details, providing credible but limited medical insight. Author Credibility: BBC Sport is a reputable source with experienced tennis journalists. Methodology: Based on match reporting, player interviews, and tournament results, offering a straightforward news account.

perspectives.description: Kartal emphasizes listening to her body and the difficulty of competing injured, reflecting athlete resilience.
